title: Production Readiness
decision_rule: The 211 service-navigation portal can ship only when every required gate is passing in the target environment or has a documented launch exception.

dependencies.name: Interaction Timeline
dependencies.validation: npm --prefix wallet_interface/ui test -- --runInBand; python -m pytest tests/test_wallet_interface_api.py -q

required_validation.name: Portal UI regression and smoke suite
Portal package parser and manifest coverage
Mobile action URL and calendar generation

release_gates.acceptance: Core service-navigation flows have desktop and mobile accessibility review with no accepted P0/P1 blockers.
release_checks: Keyboard focus and visible focus are usable on service search, detail, plan, interaction timeline, worker redaction, and offline banners.
Screen reader names are present for action buttons, filters, plan controls, revoke controls, and status banners.
Large text, mobile viewport, contrast, and reduced-motion review has no launch-blocking issue.

release_gates.acceptance: iOS Safari and Android Chrome smoke tests cover service detail, handoff actions, share/calendar fallbacks, wallet persistence, worker redaction, and offline shell behavior.
release_checks: Call, text, email, map, share, and calendar actions are generated only from valid backing data.
The browser records explicit handoff intent only and never claims provider-side completion.
Manual mobile results record browser version, device or emulator, build artifact, corpus CID, and sanitized failure notes.

release_gates.acceptance: Public service data and private wallet interaction data remain separated across UI, API, worker sharing, offline cache, analytics, and release evidence.
release_checks: Saved services, plans, notes, reminders, interactions, precise location, grants, proof receipts, uploads, and audit details are wallet-private.
Worker service-plan views render only fields allowed by active grant caveats and hide fields after revocation.
PWA caches contain public shell/detail artifacts only and bypass wallet-bearing URLs.
Release tickets, screenshots, logs, and mobile notes do not include private wallet plaintext, grant tokens, proof witnesses, secrets, or exact location.

release_gates.acceptance: HF portal package artifacts match local hashes and the release references the source retrieval package CID.
release_checks: Every post-upload file has status=ok and sha256_match=true.
Portal manifest records service, contact, location, hours, requirement, action, artifact, and coverage counts.
Any changed portal package or browser artifact is rebuilt, uploaded, and re-audited before launch.

release_gates.acceptance: Operators have documented validation, go/no-go, rollback, incident response, artifact audit, privacy review, and launch exception procedures.
release_checks: Runbook lists task validation, broader validation, troubleshooting, incident severity, rollback, artifact audit, mobile smoke, privacy review, and launch checklist.
Threat model documents trust boundaries, data classification, privacy rules, threats, mitigations, and incident handling.
Release owner records go/no-go, privacy reviewer, accessibility reviewer, artifact publisher, rollback owner, and on-call path in the target release ticket.

operator_handoff.rollback_strategy: Disable the affected portal surface, fall back to public read-only service detail when wallet workflows are unsafe, evict or bump unsafe service-worker caches, and re-run required validation before re-enabling.
